title: Strikes on Iran throw spanner in the works for inflation as oil and gas prices surge | The Business
description: With Iran attacking other nations' energy infrastructure - it is now clear the regional war has the potential for far-reaching global consequences.  While speaking at a business summit in Sydney, RBA governor Michele Bullock responded to concerns the conflict and its impacts on fuel prices could add to inflation, and lead to another interest rate rise.  "It's too early to say what the impact will be...a supply shock could, for example, add to inflation pressures and the potential implications for inflation expectations are something we are very alert to," she says.  Iran has attacked key Saudi Arabian oil infrastructure, marking a dramatic escalation in its strategy to halt the flow of oil to the West.  It's threatened to attack ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz and saying the regime says passage is closed.  One of the world's biggest gas exporters Qatar has shut down LNG production after strikes against two facilities.  A spike in gas prices four years ago caused electricity prices to soar and sent inflation into orbit,  forcing the RBA into a punishing round of interest rate hikes.  The treasurer is warning against price gouging at the petrol pump but surging gas prices could be a much bigger headache.  #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Trump warns biggest strike on Tehran 'yet to come' as conflict widens | ABC NEWS
description: US President Donald Trump has told journalists he's not afraid to send in American troops to Iran if necessary.  Meanwhile, the Iranian regime's retaliation has hit 14 countries, pounding key oil refineries and shutting down the strait of Hormuz. title: Israel military deploys more troops to southern Lebanon to fend off Hezbollah | The World | ABC NEWS
description: The Israel Defense Forces have announced they are deploying troops to points in southern Lebanon, marking a major escalation in its campaign against the Iran-backed group Hezbollah.  It comes after Hezbollah fired projectiles at Israel, in a move it says was "defensive", prompting the Israeli military to conduct strikes in Lebanon against what it says were Hezbollah sites.A spokesperson for the IDF says the troop deployment to the "borderline area" was to "enhance forward defence".  Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the latest developments. title: Is the US running short of munitions to fend off Iranian retaliation? | The World | ABC News
description: The US has projected the war in the Middle East will last four to five weeks, but it could be longer. President Trump says a "big wave" of joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran is yet to come. The potential for a protracted conflict is raising fresh concerns for the US and Israel - a dwindling supply of munitions. Aaron Stein from the Foreign Policy Research Institute spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the affects of the conflict on military stockpiles.
